La distribution solide-liquide du fer dans le manteau profond de la Terre

tu.prop: Melting processes in the deep mantle have important implications for the origin of the deep-derived plumes believed to feed hotspot volcanoes such as those in Hawaii1.

tuv.seg: Abstract
Résumé

tu.prop: They also provide insight into how the mantle has evolved, geochemically and dynamically, since the formation of Earth2.

tuv.seg: Melting processes in the deep mantle have important implications for the origin of the deep-derived plumes believed to feed hotspot volcanoes such as those in Hawaii<g id="1">1</g>.
Les processus de fusion dans le manteau profond ont une grande incidence sur l'origine des panaches profonds supposés alimenter les volcans de point chaud tels que ceux d'Hawaï.

tu.prop: Melting processes in the deep mantle have important implications for the origin of the deep-derived plumes believed to feed hotspot volcanoes such as those in Hawaii1.
Melt production in the shallow mantle is quite well understood, but deeper melting near the core–mantle boundary remains controversial.

tuv.seg: They also provide insight into how the mantle has evolved, geochemically and dynamically, since the formation of Earth<g id="2">2</g>.
Ces processus permettent également de mieux comprendre l'évolution géochimique et dynamique du manteau depuis la formation de la Terre.

tu.prop: They also provide insight into how the mantle has evolved, geochemically and dynamically, since the formation of Earth2.
Modelling the dynamic behaviour of deep, partially molten mantle requires knowledge of the density contrast between solid and melt fractions.

tuv.seg: Melt production in the shallow mantle is quite well understood, but deeper melting near the core–mantle boundary remains controversial.
La production de fusion dans le manteau peu profond est assez bien comprise, mais la fusion plus profonde près de la limite noyau-manteau reste un sujet de controverse.

tu.prop: Melt production in the shallow mantle is quite well understood, but deeper melting near the core–mantle boundary remains controversial.

Although both positive and negative melt buoyancies can produce major chemical segregation between different geochemical reservoirs, each type of buoyancy yields drastically different geodynamical models.

tuv.seg: Modelling the dynamic behaviour of deep, partially molten mantle requires knowledge of the density contrast between solid and melt fractions.
La modélisation du comportement dynamique du manteau profond partiellement fondu requiert la connaissance du contraste de densité entre les fractions solides et les fractions de liquides.

tu.prop: Modelling the dynamic behaviour of deep, partially molten mantle requires knowledge of the density contrast between solid and melt fractions.
Ascent or descent of liquids in a partially molten deep mantle should contribute to surface volcanism or production of a deep magma ocean, respectively.

tuv.seg: Although both positive and negative melt buoyancies can produce major chemical segregation between different geochemical reservoirs, each type of buoyancy yields drastically different geodynamical models.

Les flottabilités positives et négatives de la fusion peuvent entraîner une ségrégation chimique majeure entre les différents réservoirs géochimiques, et ce, malgré le fait que chaque type de flottabilité soulève des modèles géodynamiques radicalement différents.

tu.prop: Although both positive and negative melt buoyancies can produce major chemical segregation between different geochemical reservoirs, each type of buoyancy yields drastically different geodynamical models.
We investigated phase relations in a partially molten chondritic-type material under deep-mantle conditions.

tuv.seg: Ascent or descent of liquids in a partially molten deep mantle should contribute to surface volcanism or production of a deep magma ocean, respectively.
La remontée ou la descente de liquides dans un manteau profond partiellement fondu devrait contribuer, respectivement, au volcanisme de surface ou à la production d'un océan de magma profond.

tu.prop: Ascent or descent of liquids in a partially molten deep mantle should contribute to surface volcanism or production of a deep magma ocean, respectively.

Here we show that the iron partition coefficient between aluminium-bearing (Mg,Fe)SiO3 perovskite and liquid is between 0.45 and 0.6, so iron is not as incompatible with deep-mantle minerals as has been reported previously3.

tuv.seg: We investigated phase relations in a partially molten chondritic-type material under deep-mantle conditions.
Nous avons étudié les relations de phases dans la matière de type chondritique en fusion partielle dans les conditions du manteau profond.

tu.prop: We investigated phase relations in a partially molten chondritic-type material under deep-mantle conditions.
Calculated solid and melt density contrasts suggest that melt generated at the core–mantle boundary should be buoyant, and hence should segregate upwards.

tuv.seg: Here we show that the iron partition coefficient between aluminium-bearing (Mg,Fe)SiO<g id="3">3</g> perovskite and liquid is between 0.45 and 0.6, so iron is not as incompatible with deep-mantle minerals as has been reported previously<g id="4">3</g>.

Nous démontrons ici que le coefficient de distribution du fer entre la pérovskite contenant de l'aluminium (Mg, Fe)SiO3 et le liquide se situe entre 0.45 et 0.6. Par conséquent, le fer n'est pas incompatible avec les minéraux du manteau profond, comme cela avait été constaté précédemment.

tu.prop: Here we show that the iron partition coefficient between aluminium-bearing (Mg,Fe)SiO3 perovskite and liquid is between 0.45 and 0.6, so iron is not as incompatible with deep-mantle minerals as has been reported previously3.

In the framework of the magma oceans induced by large meteoritic impacts on early Earth, our results imply that the magma crystallization should push the liquids towards the surface and form a deep solid residue depleted in incompatible elements.

tuv.seg: Calculated solid and melt density contrasts suggest that melt generated at the core–mantle boundary should be buoyant, and hence should segregate upwards.
Le calcul des contrastes de densité solide-liquide suggère que la fusion produite à la limite manteau-noyau devrait être flottante, et devrait ainsi se séparer vers le haut.

tu.prop: Calculated solid and melt density contrasts suggest that melt generated at the core–mantle boundary should be buoyant, and hence should segregate upwards.
Similar content being viewed by others

tuv.seg: In the framework of the magma oceans induced by large meteoritic impacts on early Earth, our results imply that the magma crystallization should push the liquids towards the surface and form a deep solid residue depleted in incompatible elements.

Dans la structure des océans de magma engendrés par des impacts météoritiques de grande envergure sur la Terre primitive, nos résultats suggèrent simplement que la cristallisation du magma devrait pousser les liquides vers la surface et former un profond résidu solide diminué dans les éléments incompatibles.

tu.prop: In the framework of the magma oceans induced by large meteoritic impacts on early Earth, our results imply that the magma crystallization should push the liquids towards the surface and form a deep solid residue depleted in incompatible elements.

tu.prop: Zones in the lowermost mantle where seismic waves have ultra-low velocities have been interpreted as indicative of partial melting1.

tuv.seg: Main
Principal

tu.prop: Between 5% and 30% partial melting could reduce the velocities of P and S seismic waves by between 10% and 30% for specific geometries of liquid domains in the solid–liquid mixture, in agreement with seismological observations4,5.

tuv.seg: Zones in the lowermost mantle where seismic waves have ultra-low velocities have been interpreted as indicative of partial melting<g id="1">1</g>.
Les zones du manteau basal où les ondes sismiques ont des vitesses extrêmement faibles ont été interprétées comme indiquant une fusion<g id="1"></g> partielle.

tu.prop: Zones in the lowermost mantle where seismic waves have ultra-low velocities have been interpreted as indicative of partial melting1.
Such values represent a high degree of partial melting in comparison with the approximately 10–15% observed in the shallow mantle beneath mid-ocean ridges.

tuv.seg: Between 5% and 30% partial melting could reduce the velocities of P and S seismic waves by between 10% and 30% for specific geometries of liquid domains in the solid–liquid mixture, in agreement with seismological observations<g id="2">4</g><g id="3">,<g id="4">5</g></g>.

Une fusion partielle de 5 à 30 % pourrait réduire la vitesse des ondes sismiques P et S de 10 à 30 % pour des géométries spécifiques de domaines liquides dans le mélange solide-liquide, en accord avec les observations sismologiques.

tu.prop: Between 5% and 30% partial melting could reduce the velocities of P and S seismic waves by between 10% and 30% for specific geometries of liquid domains in the solid–liquid mixture, in agreement with seismological observations4,5.
Furthermore, this value for ridges is the result of an integration over a depth interval of 30–40 km of locally very low degrees of partial melting: less than 1% (ref.

tuv.seg: Such values represent a high degree of partial melting in comparison with the approximately 10–15% observed in the shallow mantle beneath mid-ocean ridges.
Ces valeurs représentent un degré élevé de fusion partielle par rapport aux quelque 10 à 15 % observés dans le manteau peu profond sous les dorsales médio-océaniques.

tu.prop: Such values represent a high degree of partial melting in comparison with the approximately 10–15% observed in the shallow mantle beneath mid-ocean ridges.

tuv.seg: Furthermore, this value for ridges is the result of an integration over a depth interval of 30–40 km of locally very low degrees of partial melting: less than 1% (ref.
De plus, cette valeur pour les dorsales est le résultat d'une intégration sur un intervalle de profondeur de 30-40 km de degrés de fusion partielle localement très faibles : moins de 1 % (réf.

tu.prop: Furthermore, this value for ridges is the result of an integration over a depth interval of 30–40 km of locally very low degrees of partial melting: less than 1% (ref.
If partial melting were that high in the lowermost mantle, the consequences for the formation of distinct geochemical reservoirs would be major7.

Deep-mantle seismic features could also be attributable to heterogeneities in the purely solid phases, produced by core–mantle chemical interactions or the presence of foreign materials introduced by large-scale mantle convection8,9.

tuv.seg: If partial melting were that high in the lowermost mantle, the consequences for the formation of distinct geochemical reservoirs would be major<g id="6">7</g>.
Si la fusion partielle était aussi élevée dans le manteau basal, les conséquences seraient considérables pour la formation de différents réservoirs géochimiques.

tu.prop: If partial melting were that high in the lowermost mantle, the consequences for the formation of distinct geochemical reservoirs would be major7.

Although recent estimations of solidus melting temperature converge on 4,150 K at the core–mantle boundary (CMB) pressure of 135 GPa (refs 10, 11), our knowledge of the temperature in this region remains too vague to assert melting in the lowermost mantle.

tuv.seg: Deep-mantle seismic features could also be attributable to heterogeneities in the purely solid phases, produced by core–mantle chemical interactions or the presence of foreign materials introduced by large-scale mantle convection<g id="7">8</g><g id="8">,<g id="9">9</g></g>.

Les caractéristiques sismiques du manteau profond pourraient aussi être imputables aux hétérogénéités dans les phases exclusivement solides. Celles-ci sont générées par les interactions chimiques de la limite noyau-manteau ou par la présence de matériaux étrangers introduits par la convection mantellique à grande échelle.

tu.prop: Deep-mantle seismic features could also be attributable to heterogeneities in the purely solid phases, produced by core–mantle chemical interactions or the presence of foreign materials introduced by large-scale mantle convection8,9.

The major parameters controlling the buoyancy of deep-mantle melts are: the changes in atomic packing between solid and liquid phases that can contribute to a volume increase of 3–4% (refs 12, 13); the iron partitioning coefficient between solid and liquid mantle fractions; and the MgO/SiO2 ratio in the liquid phase14.

tuv.seg: Although recent estimations of solidus melting temperature converge on 4,150 K at the core–mantle boundary (CMB) pressure of 135 GPa (refs <g id="10">10</g>, <g id="11">11</g>), our knowledge of the temperature in this region remains too vague to assert melting in the lowermost mantle.

Bien que les estimations récentes de la température de fusion du solidus convergent vers 4 150 K à une pression de 135 GPa à la frontière noyau-manteau (FNM) (références <g id="10">10</g>, <g id="11">11</g>), notre connaissance de la température dans cette région reste trop vague pour affirmer la fusion dans le manteau basal.

tu.prop: Although recent estimations of solidus melting temperature converge on 4,150 K at the core–mantle boundary (CMB) pressure of 135 GPa (refs 10, 11), our knowledge of the temperature in this region remains too vague to assert melting in the lowermost mantle.

We carried out experiments to determine these parameters more accurately, using synthetic (Ca,Mg,Al,Si,Fe) glass with a composition that modelled the primitive mantle after core segregation (similar to pyrolite, and the same sample as used previously10).

tuv.seg: The major parameters controlling the buoyancy of deep-mantle melts are: the changes in atomic packing between solid and liquid phases that can contribute to a volume increase of 3–4% (refs <g id="1">12</g>, <g id="2">13</g>); the iron partitioning coefficient between solid and liquid mantle fractions; and the MgO/SiO<g id="3">2</g> ratio in the liquid phase<g id="4">14</g>.

Les paramètres majeurs contrôlant la flottabilité des fusions du manteau profond sont : les changements dans l'empilement atomique entre les phases solides et liquides qui peuvent contribuer à une augmentation de 3-4 % du volume (références 12, 13) ; le coefficient de distribution du fer entre les fractions solides et liquides du manteau ; et le rapport MgO/SiO2 dans la phase liquide.

tu.prop: The major parameters controlling the buoyancy of deep-mantle melts are: the changes in atomic packing between solid and liquid phases that can contribute to a volume increase of 3–4% (refs 12, 13); the iron partitioning coefficient between solid and liquid mantle fractions; and the MgO/SiO2 ratio in the liquid phase14.
We did not include minor and trace elements, because their effect on iron partitioning can be neglected to a first approximation.

tuv.seg: We carried out experiments to determine these parameters more accurately, using synthetic (Ca,Mg,Al,Si,Fe) glass with a composition that modelled the primitive mantle after core segregation (similar to pyrolite, and the same sample as used previously<g id="5">10</g>).

Nous avons mené des expériences pour déterminer plus précisément ces paramètres, en utilisant du verre synthétique (Ca, Mg, Al, Si, Fe) avec une composition qui représente le manteau primitif après ségrégation du noyau (semblable à la pyrolite, et au même échantillon utilisé précédemment).

tu.prop: We carried out experiments to determine these parameters more accurately, using synthetic (Ca,Mg,Al,Si,Fe) glass with a composition that modelled the primitive mantle after core segregation (similar to pyrolite, and the same sample as used previously10).
We did the experiments in a diamond-anvil cell (DAC) at pressures from 40 GPa to 120 GPa, using very thin (5–10 µm) samples that were melted throughout their thickness using infrared lasers (Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: We did not include minor and trace elements, because their effect on iron partitioning can be neglected to a first approximation.
Cependant, nous n'avons pas inclus d'éléments mineurs ou d'éléments traces, car leur effet sur la distribution du fer peut être négligeable pour une première estimation.

tu.prop: We did not include minor and trace elements, because their effect on iron partitioning can be neglected to a first approximation.
Melting criteria are based on the use of in situ X-ray diffraction, and details are reported elsewhere10.

tuv.seg: We did the experiments in a diamond-anvil cell (DAC) at pressures from 40 GPa to 120 GPa, using very thin (5–10 µm) samples that were melted throughout their thickness using infrared lasers (<g id="6">Fig. 1</g>).

Nous également avons réalisé ces expériences dans une cellule à enclumes de diamant (CED) à des pressions allant de 40 à 120 GPa, en utilisant de très petits échantillons (5–10 µm) que nous avons fait fondre sur toute leur épaisseur à l'aide de lasers infrarouges (<g id="6">Figure 1</g>).

tu.prop: We did the experiments in a diamond-anvil cell (DAC) at pressures from 40 GPa to 120 GPa, using very thin (5–10 µm) samples that were melted throughout their thickness using infrared lasers (Fig. 1).
We analysed the recovered samples using simultaneous X-ray diffraction (XRD) and X-ray fluorescence (XRF) (Fig. 2), generating XRD and XRF maps with resolutions up to around 500 nm (Table 1).

tuv.seg: Melting criteria are based on the use of <g id="7">in situ</g> X-ray diffraction, and details are reported elsewhere<g id="8">10</g>.
Les critères de fusion sont déterminés à partir de l'utilisation in situ de la diffraction des rayons X, et des détails de l'expérience sont présentés ailleurs.

tu.prop: Melting criteria are based on the use of in situ X-ray diffraction, and details are reported elsewhere10.
We successfully extracted some samples from their gaskets, and analysed them chemically using the electron microprobe (see Methods and Supplementary Information).

tuv.seg: We analysed the recovered samples using simultaneous X-ray diffraction (XRD) and X-ray fluorescence (XRF) (<g id="9">Fig. 2</g>), generating XRD and XRF maps with resolutions up to around 500 nm (<g id="10">Table 1</g>).

Ensuite, nous avons analysé les échantillons obtenus en utilisant à la fois la diffraction des rayons X (diffractométrie X) et la fluorescence des rayons X (fluorescence X) (<g id="9">Figure 2</g>), ce qui a permis de créer des cartographies de diffractométrie et de fluorescence X à une résolution allant jusqu'à environ 500 nm (<g id="10">Tableau 1</g>).

tu.prop: We analysed the recovered samples using simultaneous X-ray diffraction (XRD) and X-ray fluorescence (XRF) (Fig. 2), generating XRD and XRF maps with resolutions up to around 500 nm (Table 1).

tuv.seg: We successfully extracted some samples from their gaskets, and analysed them chemically using the electron microprobe (see Methods and <g id="11">Supplementary Information</g>).

Nous avons réussi à extraire quelques échantillons de leurs joints et à les analyser chimiquement à l'aide d'une microsonde électronique (voir sections Méthodes et <g id="11">Informations supplémentaires</g>)

tu.prop: We successfully extracted some samples from their gaskets, and analysed them chemically using the electron microprobe (see Methods and Supplementary Information).

Exemples de données expérimentales brutes.

tuv.seg: After samples were melted and pressure released, we took XRD measurements at several sample positions.
Après la fusion des échantillons et le relâchement de la pression, nous avons effectué des mesures de diffractométrie X à plusieurs points de l'échantillon.

tu.prop: After samples were melted and pressure released, we took XRD measurements at several sample positions.
The calcium silicate perovskite is not visible in this sample region.

tuv.seg: Typical spectrum (<g id="1">a</g>) shows a mixture of Mg-Pv (blue), Fp (orange) and the NaCl (green) pressure medium.
Le spectre typique (<g id="1">a</g>) indique un mélange de Mg-Pv (en bleu), Fp (en orange) et la pression moyenne du NaCl (en vert).

tu.prop: Typical spectrum (a) shows a mixture of Mg-Pv (blue), Fp (orange) and the NaCl (green) pressure medium.
We also recorded XRF measurements at the same sample positions (b).

tuv.seg: The calcium silicate perovskite is not visible in this sample region.
Le pérovskite de silicate de calcium n'est pas visible dans cette zone de l'échantillon.

tu.prop: The calcium silicate perovskite is not visible in this sample region.
Visible peaks arise from Cl in NaCl (blue), Ca (light green) and Fe (pink) in the sample and W (dark green) in the gasket.

tuv.seg: We also recorded XRF measurements at the same sample positions (<g id="2">b</g>).
Nous avons aussi enregistré les mesures de la diffractométrie X aux mêmes points de l'échantillon (<g id="2">b</g>).

tu.prop: We also recorded XRF measurements at the same sample positions (b).
Refinement of XRF spectra provides quantitative maps of Fe content and qualitative maps for the lighter elements (See Supplementary Information).

tuv.seg: Visible peaks arise from Cl in NaCl (blue), Ca (light green) and Fe (pink) in the sample and W (dark green) in the gasket.
Dans cet échantillon, on observe des pics de Cl dans le NaCl (en bleu), de Ca (en vert clair) et de Fe (en rose) et des pics de W (en vert foncé) dans le joint.

tu.prop: Visible peaks arise from Cl in NaCl (blue), Ca (light green) and Fe (pink) in the sample and W (dark green) in the gasket.

tuv.seg: Refinement of XRF spectra provides quantitative maps of Fe content and qualitative maps for the lighter elements (See <g id="3">Supplementary Information</g>).

L’affinement du spectre de la fluorescence X a permis d'obtenir des cartographies quantitatives sur la teneur en Fe et des cartographies qualitatives pour les autres éléments (voir section <g id="3">Informations supplémentaires</g>).

tu.prop: Refinement of XRF spectra provides quantitative maps of Fe content and qualitative maps for the lighter elements (See Supplementary Information).

tu.prop: Multiphase Rietveld refinements of each XRD pattern provide maps of phase contents for each pixel in the two-dimensional maps.

tuv.seg: The XRD maps (<g id="1">Fig. 3b, d</g>) reveal circular zoning around the centre of the laser hotspot (CLHS).
Les spectres de la diffractométrie X (<g id="1">Figure 3b, d</g>) révèlent un zonage circulaire entourant le centre du rayon laser (CRL).

tu.prop: The XRD maps (Fig. 3b, d) reveal circular zoning around the centre of the laser hotspot (CLHS).
XRF spectra recorded at the same sample positions show chemical zoning with a circular shape around the CLHS, similar to the XRD features (Figs 3a, c and Supplementary Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: Multiphase Rietveld refinements of each XRD pattern provide maps of phase contents for each pixel in the two-dimensional maps.
Les affinements Rietveld multiphasiques de tous les diagrammes de diffractométrie X permettent d'obtenir des cartes des contenus de phase pour chaque pixel des cartes bidimensionnelles.

tu.prop: Multiphase Rietveld refinements of each XRD pattern provide maps of phase contents for each pixel in the two-dimensional maps.
The amplitude of chemical heterogeneities is illustrated by profile analyses of the XRF intensities at the iron K-lines (Supplementary Figs 2–5).

tuv.seg: XRF spectra recorded at the same sample positions show chemical zoning with a circular shape around the CLHS, similar to the XRD features (<g id="2">Figs 3a, c</g> and <g id="3">Supplementary Fig. 1</g>).

Le spectre de la fluorescence X enregistré aux mêmes points de l'échantillon montre un zonage chimique de forme circulaire entourant le CRL, similaire à celui de la diffractométrie X (<g id="2">Figures 3a, c</g> et <g id="3">Figure 1 supplémentaire</g>).

tu.prop: XRF spectra recorded at the same sample positions show chemical zoning with a circular shape around the CLHS, similar to the XRD features (Figs 3a, c and Supplementary Fig. 1).
Comparison of XRD and XRF maps shows that zones with a greater content of magnesium-bearing perovskite (Mg-Pv) do not overlap with zones with higher iron content.

tuv.seg: The amplitude of chemical heterogeneities is illustrated by profile analyses of the XRF intensities at the iron K-lines (<g id="4">Supplementary Figs 2–5</g>).
L'amplitude des hétérogénéités chimiques est illustrée par les analyses du profil des intensités de la fluorescence X aux couches électroniques K du fer (<g id="4">Figures supplémentaires 2-5</g>).

tu.prop: The amplitude of chemical heterogeneities is illustrated by profile analyses of the XRF intensities at the iron K-lines (Supplementary Figs 2–5).

tuv.seg: The iron partition coefficient between melt and solid at the liquidus temperature (<g id="1">D</g><g id="2">Fe</g> = <g id="3">X</g><g id="4">Fe</g><g id="5">sol</g> / <g id="6">X</g><g id="7">Fe</g><g id="8">liq</g>, where <g id="9">X</g><g id="10">Fe</g><g id="11">sol</g> and <g id="12">X</g><g id="13">Fe</g><g id="14">liq</g> are the molar iron contents in the solid and liquid phases, respectively) is inferred from variations in iron content at different sample positions.

Le coefficient de distribution du fer entre magma et roche solide à la température du liquidus (DFe = XFesol/XFeliq, où XFesol et XFeliq sont les teneurs en fer molaire dans les phases solide et liquide respectivement) est déduit des variations de la teneur en fer à des points différents de l'échantillon.

tu.prop: The iron partition coefficient between melt and solid at the liquidus temperature (DFe = XFesol / XFeliq, where XFesol and XFeliq are the molar iron contents in the solid and liquid phases, respectively) is inferred from variations in iron content at different sample positions.

tuv.seg: Our results indicate a liquid 1.6–2.1 times richer in iron than the Mg-Pv phase under pressure conditions similar to the lower mantle.

tu.prop: Our results indicate a liquid 1.6–2.1 times richer in iron than the Mg-Pv phase under pressure conditions similar to the lower mantle.

En se fondant sur ces observations, nous pouvons modéliser les relations de phase solide-liquide dans le manteau profond en fusion partielle.

On the basis of thermodynamic calculations presented in a recent study14, we calculate that a liquid with 20 mol% FeO is buoyant in the lowermost solid mantle if the liquid SiO2 content is higher than 20 mol%.

tuv.seg: A liquid in chemical equilibrium with a pyrolitic or chondritic lower mantle with FeO of around 11 mol% should adopt an FeO content of 15–20 mol%.
Le liquide en équilibre chimique avec une teneur en FeO d'environ 11 mol % dans le manteau inférieur pyrolitique ou chondritique devrait recouper une teneur en FeO de 15 à 20 mol %.

tu.prop: A liquid in chemical equilibrium with a pyrolitic or chondritic lower mantle with FeO of around 11 mol% should adopt an FeO content of 15–20 mol%.

tuv.seg: On the basis of thermodynamic calculations presented in a recent study<g id="1">14</g>, we calculate that a liquid with 20 mol% FeO is buoyant in the lowermost solid mantle if the liquid SiO<g id="2">2</g> content is higher than 20 mol%.

Sur la base des calculs thermodynamique présentés dans une étude récente<g id="1">14</g>, nous estimons que le liquide dont la teneur en FeO est de 20 mol % présente une certaine flottabilité dans le manteau solide basal (région D) si la teneur du liquide en SiO<g id="2">2</g> est supérieure à 20 mol %.

tu.prop: On the basis of thermodynamic calculations presented in a recent study14, we calculate that a liquid with 20 mol% FeO is buoyant in the lowermost solid mantle if the liquid SiO2 content is higher than 20 mol%.

tuv.seg: Previous studies reported liquid SiO<g id="3">2</g> contents of about 40 mol% at 33 GPa (ref.

tu.prop: Therefore, 20 mol% SiO2 is too low to be achieved at lower-mantle conditions and our results for DFe support buoyant silicate liquids at all mantle depths.
An increased temperature might even accentuate the upward movements, if the liquids are generated in contact with the relatively hotter CMB26.

tuv.seg: Therefore, 20 mol% SiO<g id="6">2</g> is too low to be achieved at lower-mantle conditions and our results for <g id="7">D</g><g id="8">Fe</g> support buoyant silicate liquids at all mantle depths.

Par conséquent, une teneur en SiO<g id="6">2</g> de 20 mol % est trop faible pour atteindre les conditions du manteau inférieur. De plus, notre résultat de <g id="7">D</g><g id="8">Fe</g> confirme l'existence de liquide silicaté flottant à toutes les profondeurs du manteau.

tu.prop: Therefore, 20 mol% SiO2 is too low to be achieved at lower-mantle conditions and our results for DFe support buoyant silicate liquids at all mantle depths.
We reach a similar conclusion when other geological circumstances are considered.

tuv.seg: An increased temperature might even accentuate the upward movements, if the liquids are generated in contact with the relatively hotter CMB<g id="9">26</g>.
Une température accrue pourrait même accentuer les mouvements ascendants, si les liquides sont générés en contact avec la DMM relativement plus chaude<g id="9"> 26</g>.

tu.prop: An increased temperature might even accentuate the upward movements, if the liquids are generated in contact with the relatively hotter CMB26.
In early Earth, complete melting of a large fraction of the deep mantle would yield a liquid composition close to that of the bulk-silicate mantle: that is, with an FeO-content of about 11 mol%.

tuv.seg: We reach a similar conclusion when other geological circumstances are considered.
Nous parvenons à une conclusion similaire lorsque d'autres circonstances géologiques sont prises en compte.

tu.prop: We reach a similar conclusion when other geological circumstances are considered.
Progressive crystallization of this liquid should produce an Mg-Pv phase with an FeO content of about 6 mol%, based on the DFe value of 0.55.

tuv.seg: In early Earth, complete melting of a large fraction of the deep mantle would yield a liquid composition close to that of the bulk-silicate mantle: that is, with an FeO-content of about 11 mol%.

Dans la Terre primitive, une fusion complète d'une grande partie du manteau profond produirait une composition liquide proche de celle du manteau silicaté, c'est-à-dire une teneur en FeO d'environ 11 mol %.

tu.prop: In early Earth, complete melting of a large fraction of the deep mantle would yield a liquid composition close to that of the bulk-silicate mantle: that is, with an FeO-content of about 11 mol%.

When compared with the situation described in the previous paragraph, this solid–liquid equilibrium results in a decrease of FeO content in the liquid (from 20 mol% to 11 mol%) that is significantly larger than the decrease in the solid (from 11 mol% to 6 mol%).

tuv.seg: Progressive crystallization of this liquid should produce an Mg-Pv phase with an FeO content of about 6 mol%, based on the <g id="1">D</g><g id="2">Fe</g> value of 0.55.

La cristallisation progressive de ce liquide devrait produire une phase de péorovskite magnésienne avec une teneur en FeO d'environ 6 mol %, sur la base de la valeur <g id="1">D</g><g id="2">Fe</g> de 0,55.

tu.prop: Progressive crystallization of this liquid should produce an Mg-Pv phase with an FeO content of about 6 mol%, based on the DFe value of 0.55.
It would certainly contribute to a larger liquid buoyancy.

tuv.seg: When compared with the situation described in the previous paragraph, this solid–liquid equilibrium results in a decrease of FeO content in the liquid (from 20 mol% to 11 mol%) that is significantly larger than the decrease in the solid (from 11 mol% to 6 mol%).

Par rapport à la situation décrite au paragraphe précédent, cet équilibre solide-liquide aboutit à une baisse de la teneur en FeO dans le liquide (de 20 à 11 mol %) qui est nettement plus élevé que la baisse qui se produit dans le solide (de 11 à 6 mol %).

tu.prop: When compared with the situation described in the previous paragraph, this solid–liquid equilibrium results in a decrease of FeO content in the liquid (from 20 mol% to 11 mol%) that is significantly larger than the decrease in the solid (from 11 mol% to 6 mol%).
Accordingly, it is expected that the crystallizing Mg-Pv grains should sink in the magma ocean.

tuv.seg: It would certainly contribute to a larger liquid buoyancy.
Cela pourrait certainement contribuer à une plus forte flottabilité.

tu.prop: It would certainly contribute to a larger liquid buoyancy.
The arrival in the deepest part of the magma ocean of such material depleted in incompatible elements should make its crystallization easier, because the melting temperature is increased.

tuv.seg: Accordingly, it is expected that the crystallizing Mg-Pv grains should sink in the magma ocean.
En conséquence, on s'attend à ce que des grains de pérovskite magnésienne en cours de cristallisation coulent dans l’océan de magma.

tu.prop: Accordingly, it is expected that the crystallizing Mg-Pv grains should sink in the magma ocean.

This effect, together with the shape of melting curves in the deep mantle10, is consistent with crystallization of the magma ocean starting from the bottom and progressing to the top, during the release of heat at the mantle surface.

tuv.seg: The arrival in the deepest part of the magma ocean of such material depleted in incompatible elements should make its crystallization easier, because the melting temperature is increased.
La descente de ce matériau appauvri en éléments incompatibles vers la partie la plus profonde de l'océan de magma devrait en faciliter la cristallisation, car la température de fusion est augmentée.

tu.prop: The arrival in the deepest part of the magma ocean of such material depleted in incompatible elements should make its crystallization easier, because the melting temperature is increased.
This can produce a vertical layering of Earth's mantle, in agreement with geodynamic modelling of the magma ocean2.

tuv.seg: This effect, together with the shape of melting curves in the deep mantle<g id="3">10</g>, is consistent with crystallization of the magma ocean starting from the bottom and progressing to the top, during the release of heat at the mantle surface.

Cet effet, conjointement avec la forme des courbes de fusion dans le manteau profond<g id="3">10</g>, est conforme à la cristallisation de l’océan de magma débutant de la base et augmentant jusqu’au sommet, au cours du dégagement de chaleur à la surface du manteau.

tu.prop: This effect, together with the shape of melting curves in the deep mantle10, is consistent with crystallization of the magma ocean starting from the bottom and progressing to the top, during the release of heat at the mantle surface.
A large amount of liquid being trapped in a basal magma ocean for long geological times in the very deep mantle27 seems to be incompatible with our experimental results.

tuv.seg: This can produce a vertical layering of Earth's mantle, in agreement with geodynamic modelling of the magma ocean<g id="4">2</g>.
Cela peut produire un caractère stratiforme vertical du manteau terrestre, en accord avec la modélisation géodynamique de l’océan de magma<g id="4">2</g>.

tu.prop: This can produce a vertical layering of Earth's mantle, in agreement with geodynamic modelling of the magma ocean2.
We think that such liquids should eventually have risen.

tuv.seg: A large amount of liquid being trapped in a basal magma ocean for long geological times in the very deep mantle<g id="1">27</g> seems to be incompatible with our experimental results.

Il semble qu'une grande quantité de liquide piégé dans un océan de magma basal pendant de longues périodes géologiques dans le manteau très profond<g id="1">27</g> soit incompatible avec nos résultats expérimentaux.

tu.prop: A large amount of liquid being trapped in a basal magma ocean for long geological times in the very deep mantle27 seems to be incompatible with our experimental results.
Also, the origin of such deep liquids can hardly be related to the early occurrence of magma oceans at Earth's surface, because silicate liquids are not expected to sink towards the CMB.

tuv.seg: We think that such liquids should eventually have risen.
Par conséquent, nous pensons que ces liquides auraient dû finir par monter.

tu.prop: We think that such liquids should eventually have risen.
Consequently, such a dense basal magma ocean, if it existed, should be preferentially related to the existence of a very hot core, inducing partial melting in the lowermost mantle.

tuv.seg: Also, the origin of such deep liquids can hardly be related to the early occurrence of magma oceans at Earth's surface, because silicate liquids are not expected to sink towards the CMB.

De plus, l'origine de ces liquides profonds peut difficilement s'associer à l'occurrence primitive de l'océan de magma à la surface terrestre, parce que les liquides silicatés ne sont pas censés couler vers la limite noyau-manteau.

tu.prop: Also, the origin of such deep liquids can hardly be related to the early occurrence of magma oceans at Earth's surface, because silicate liquids are not expected to sink towards the CMB.
This situation could have persisted for long geological times, independent of the episodic mantle melting induced by meteoritic impacts at Earth's surface.

tuv.seg: Consequently, such a dense basal magma ocean, if it existed, should be preferentially related to the existence of a very hot core, inducing partial melting in the lowermost mantle.
En conséquence, un océan de magma basal, s'il existait, devrait être lié de préférence à l'existence d'un noyau très chaud, impliquant une fusion partielle dans le manteau basal.

tu.prop: Consequently, such a dense basal magma ocean, if it existed, should be preferentially related to the existence of a very hot core, inducing partial melting in the lowermost mantle.

tuv.seg: This situation could have persisted for long geological times, independent of the episodic mantle melting induced by meteoritic impacts at Earth's surface.
Cette situation aurait pu persister pendant de longues périodes géologiques, indépendamment de la fonte épisodique du manteau induite par les impacts météoritiques à la surface de la Terre.

tu.prop: This situation could have persisted for long geological times, independent of the episodic mantle melting induced by meteoritic impacts at Earth's surface.
Very thin (5–10 µm) samples were loaded into the DAC between two NaCl pellets of 5– µm thickness each.

tuv.seg: Methods Summary
Résumé des méthodes

tu.prop: The use of relatively thick NaCl pellets minimizes the axial temperature gradient and allows the sample to melt throughout its thickness.

tuv.seg: Very thin (5–10 µm) samples were loaded into the DAC between two NaCl pellets of 5– µm thickness each.
La cellule à enclumes de diamant (CED) a été chargée d'échantillons très fins (5-10 µm3) placés entre deux pastilles NaCl d'une épaisseur de 5-10 µm chacune.

tu.prop: Very thin (5–10 µm) samples were loaded into the DAC between two NaCl pellets of 5– µm thickness each.
After compression to the target pressure, samples were heated by two infrared lasers with a spot diameter of more than 20 µm.

tuv.seg: The use of relatively thick NaCl pellets minimizes the axial temperature gradient and allows the sample to melt throughout its thickness.

Ces pastilles NaCl, dont l'épaisseur est relativement importante, permettait de minimiser le gradient de température dans l'axe de l'échantillon qui, par conséquent, a fondu uniformément dans toute son épaisseur.

tu.prop: The use of relatively thick NaCl pellets minimizes the axial temperature gradient and allows the sample to melt throughout its thickness.
Temperature was measured by analysing the pyrometric signal emitted over a sample area of 3 µm × 3 µm.

tuv.seg: After compression to the target pressure, samples were heated by two infrared lasers with a spot diameter of more than 20 µm.
Une fois que le niveau de pression désiré était atteint, les échantillons ont été chauffés par deux lasers infrarouges dont le diamètre du spot était supérieur à 20 µm.

tu.prop: After compression to the target pressure, samples were heated by two infrared lasers with a spot diameter of more than 20 µm.
Sample melting was achieved for durations ranging from a couple of seconds to around 1 min before rapid quenching.

tuv.seg: Temperature was measured by analysing the pyrometric signal emitted over a sample area of 3 µm × 3 µm.
La température a été mesurée selon une analyse de signal pyrométrique dans une zone d'échantillon de 3 µm × 3 µm.

tu.prop: Temperature was measured by analysing the pyrometric signal emitted over a sample area of 3 µm × 3 µm.

For the diffraction measurements, the X-ray beam of the ID27 beamline at the European Synchrotron Radiation Facility (ESRF) was tuned to 33 keV and focused by two Kirkpatrick–Baez mirrors to less than 2 µm × 2 µm on the sample.

tuv.seg: Sample melting was achieved for durations ranging from a couple of seconds to around 1 min before rapid quenching.
Pour atteindre la fusion des échantillons, il a fallu entre quelques secondes et une minute avant une trempe rapide.

tu.prop: Sample melting was achieved for durations ranging from a couple of seconds to around 1 min before rapid quenching.
We used the MAR-160 charge-coupled-device detector with a typical acquisition time of 20–30 s. Multiphase Rietveld refinements provided phase contents of Mg-Pv and Ca-bearing perovskite and Fp.

tuv.seg: For the diffraction measurements, the X-ray beam of the ID27 beamline at the European Synchrotron Radiation Facility (ESRF) was tuned to 33 keV and focused by two Kirkpatrick–Baez mirrors to less than 2 µm × 2 µm on the sample.

Pour mesurer la diffraction, le faisceau de rayons X de la ligne de faisceaux ID27 au European Synchrotron Radiation Facility (ESRF) a été calibré à 33 KeV et focalisé sur l'échantillon à l'aide de deux miroirs Kirkpatrick-Baez dans un champ inférieur à 2 µm x 2 µm.

tu.prop: For the diffraction measurements, the X-ray beam of the ID27 beamline at the European Synchrotron Radiation Facility (ESRF) was tuned to 33 keV and focused by two Kirkpatrick–Baez mirrors to less than 2 µm × 2 µm on the sample.
The diffraction signal decreases away from the CLHS, where the glassy starting material was not heated enough for it to crystallize.

tuv.seg: We used the MAR-160 charge-coupled-device detector with a typical acquisition time of 20–30 s. Multiphase Rietveld refinements provided phase contents of Mg-Pv and Ca-bearing perovskite and Fp.

Nous avons utilisé le détecteur à dispositif à transfert de charge MAR-160 avec un temps d'acquisition typique de 20 à 30 secondes. L'affinement Rietveld multiphasique a permis de déterminer les teneurs en phases de la pérovskite magnésienne, de la pérovskite contenant du calcium et de la pérovskite ferreuse.

tu.prop: We used the MAR-160 charge-coupled-device detector with a typical acquisition time of 20–30 s. Multiphase Rietveld refinements provided phase contents of Mg-Pv and Ca-bearing perovskite and Fp.
X-ray fluorescence analysis was done at the ESRF’s ID27 and ID21 beamlines.

tuv.seg: The diffraction signal decreases away from the CLHS, where the glassy starting material was not heated enough for it to crystallize.
Le signal de diffraction diminue à mesure qu'on s'éloigne du CRL, où le matériel vitreux initial n'a pas été suffisamment chauffé pour se cristalliser.

tu.prop: The diffraction signal decreases away from the CLHS, where the glassy starting material was not heated enough for it to crystallize.
All reported measurements were taken after release of pressure and temperature.

tuv.seg: X-ray fluorescence analysis was done at the ESRF’s ID27 and ID21 beamlines.
L'analyse par fluorescence X a été effectuée aux lignes de faisceaux ID27 et ID21 de l'ESRF.

tu.prop: X-ray fluorescence analysis was done at the ESRF’s ID27 and ID21 beamlines.
We used different geometries with the detector located at about 60° or 90° from the incident beam.

tuv.seg: All reported measurements were taken after release of pressure and temperature.
Toutes les mesures rapportées ont été prises après libération de la pression et de la température.

tu.prop: All reported measurements were taken after release of pressure and temperature.
For some experiments, we used a polycapillary X-ray half-lens to enhance the XRF signal–noise ratio.

tuv.seg: We used different geometries with the detector located at about 60° or 90° from the incident beam.
Nous avons utilisé des géométries différentes avec le détecteur situé à une distance entre 60° et 90° du faisceau incident.

tu.prop: We used different geometries with the detector located at about 60° or 90° from the incident beam.
At ID21, the monochromatic beam was tuned to 7.2 keV and focused to 0.2 µm × 0.7 µm using a Fresnel Zone Plate lens.

tuv.seg: For some experiments, we used a polycapillary X-ray half-lens to enhance the XRF signal–noise ratio.
Pour certaines expériences, nous avons utilisé une demi-lentille polycapillaire à rayons X afin d'améliorer le rapport signal-bruit de la fluorescence X.

tu.prop: For some experiments, we used a polycapillary X-ray half-lens to enhance the XRF signal–noise ratio.
Typical acquisition times were 100–150 s per spectrum at ID27 and 1 s per spectrum at ID21.

tuv.seg: At ID21, the monochromatic beam was tuned to 7.2 keV and focused to 0.2 µm × 0.7 µm using a Fresnel Zone Plate lens.
À l'ID21, le faisceau monochromatique a été porté à une tension de 7,2 keV et focalisé à 0,2 µm × 0,7 µm en utilisant une lentille de plaque à zone de Fresnel.

tu.prop: At ID21, the monochromatic beam was tuned to 7.2 keV and focused to 0.2 µm × 0.7 µm using a Fresnel Zone Plate lens.
We detected K-lines of Ca and Fe from the sample, the K-line of Cl from the NaCl pellets and L-lines of W or Re from the gasket material.

tuv.seg: Typical acquisition times were 100–150 s per spectrum at ID27 and 1 s per spectrum at ID21.
Les temps d'acquisition typiques étaient de 100 à 150 s par spectre à l'ID27 et de 1 s par spectre à l'ID21.

tu.prop: Typical acquisition times were 100–150 s per spectrum at ID27 and 1 s per spectrum at ID21.
We could not measure Mg, Si and Ca fluorescence with sufficient accuracy to draw major consequences for their repartition within the melted regions.

tuv.seg: We detected K-lines of Ca and Fe from the sample, the K-line of Cl from the NaCl pellets and L-lines of W or Re from the gasket material.
Nous avons détecté des seuils K du Ca et du Fe pour l'échantillon, un seuil K du Cl pour les pastilles NaCl et des seuils L du W ou du Re pour le matériel de joint.

tu.prop: We detected K-lines of Ca and Fe from the sample, the K-line of Cl from the NaCl pellets and L-lines of W or Re from the gasket material.

tuv.seg: We could not measure Mg, Si and Ca fluorescence with sufficient accuracy to draw major consequences for their repartition within the melted regions.

Nous n'avons pas pu mesurer la fluorescence de Mp, Si et Ca de manière suffisamment précise pour en déterminer les conséquences majeures de la répartition de ces éléments dans les régions ayant subi une fusion.

tu.prop: We could not measure Mg, Si and Ca fluorescence with sufficient accuracy to draw major consequences for their repartition within the melted regions.

tuv.seg: Online Methods
Méthodes en ligne

tu.prop: We used a synthetic (Ca,Mg,Al,Si,Fe) glass with oxide contents in chondritic proportions, except for iron with a Fe/(Σ cations) ratio of 6.1.

tuv.seg: Sample preparation
Préparation des échantillons

tu.prop: This represents a model composition for the primitive mantle after core segregation (similar to pyrolite, and the same sample as in ref.

tuv.seg: We used a synthetic (Ca,Mg,Al,Si,Fe) glass with oxide contents in chondritic proportions, except for iron with a Fe/(Σ cations) ratio of 6.1.
Nous avons utilisé du verre synthétique (Ca, Mg, Al, Si, Fe) dont les matériaux chondritiques contenaient de l'oxyde, sauf pour le fer, dont le ratio Fe / (Σ cations) était de 6,1.

tu.prop: We used a synthetic (Ca,Mg,Al,Si,Fe) glass with oxide contents in chondritic proportions, except for iron with a Fe/(Σ cations) ratio of 6.1.

tuv.seg: This represents a model composition for the primitive mantle after core segregation (similar to pyrolite, and the same sample as in ref.
Cette composition sert de modèle pour le manteau primitif après la ségrégation du noyau (semblable au pyrolite ; le même échantillon que dans ref.

tu.prop: This represents a model composition for the primitive mantle after core segregation (similar to pyrolite, and the same sample as in ref.
We did not include minor and trace elements, among which the most abundant are Na (4,900 p.p.m.) and K (560 p.p.m.), because their effect on Fe partitioning can be neglected to a first approximation.
We performed experiments at high pressure and high temperature using the laser-heated diamond anvil cell (LH-DAC) technique.

tuv.seg: We did not include minor and trace elements, among which the most abundant are Na (4,900 p.p.m.) and K (560 p.p.m.), because their effect on Fe partitioning can be neglected to a first approximation.

Cependant, nous n'avons pas inclus d'éléments mineurs ou d'éléments traces, dont les plus nombreux sont Na (4 900 p.p.m.) et K (560 p.p.m.), car leur effet sur la distribution du fer peut être négligeable pour une première estimation.

tu.prop: We did not include minor and trace elements, among which the most abundant are Na (4,900 p.p.m.) and K (560 p.p.m.), because their effect on Fe partitioning can be neglected to a first approximation.
We used diamond anvils with flat culets of 250-µm diameter or bevel-type culets of 100–300-µm diameter.

tuv.seg: We performed experiments at high pressure and high temperature using the laser-heated diamond anvil cell (LH-DAC) technique.
Nos expériences ont été effectuées à haute pression et à haute température en utilisant la technique de cellule à enclumes de diamant (CED) chauffé par laser.

tu.prop: We performed experiments at high pressure and high temperature using the laser-heated diamond anvil cell (LH-DAC) technique.
Very thin (5–10 µm) samples were loaded in tungsten or rhenium gaskets between two NaCl pellets of 5–10-µm thickness each.

tuv.seg: We used diamond anvils with flat culets of 250-µm diameter or bevel-type culets of 100–300-µm diameter.
Nous avons utilisé des cellules à enclumes de diamant dont les culets étaient plats (250 µm en diamètre) ou à angles obliques (100-300 µm en diamètre).

tu.prop: We used diamond anvils with flat culets of 250-µm diameter or bevel-type culets of 100–300-µm diameter.
After we compressed the sample to the target pressure, we heated the samples using two Nd-doped Y-Al-garnet or Y-fibre lasers with a laser-spot size of more than 20-µm diameter.

tuv.seg: Very thin (5–10 µm) samples were loaded in tungsten or rhenium gaskets between two NaCl pellets of 5–10-µm thickness each.
Des joints en tungstène ou en rhénium ont été chargés d'échantillons fins placés entre deux pastilles NaCl d'une épaisseur de 5-10 µm chacune.

tu.prop: Very thin (5–10 µm) samples were loaded in tungsten or rhenium gaskets between two NaCl pellets of 5–10-µm thickness each.
The temperature was measured at the centre of the laser hotspot by analysing the pyrometric signal emitted over a sample area of 3 × 3 µm2.

tuv.seg: After we compressed the sample to the target pressure, we heated the samples using two Nd-doped Y-Al-garnet or Y-fibre lasers with a laser-spot size of more than 20-µm diameter.

Une fois que nous avons comprimé l'échantillon au niveau de pression désiré, nous avons chauffé les échantillons avec deux lasers (soit un laser Nd : YAG soit un laser à fibre Y) dont le diamètre du spot laser était supérieur à 20-µm.

tu.prop: After we compressed the sample to the target pressure, we heated the samples using two Nd-doped Y-Al-garnet or Y-fibre lasers with a laser-spot size of more than 20-µm diameter.
Melting of samples throughout their entire thickness was induced by increasing laser power until the liquidus temperature was reached at the centre of the sample.

tuv.seg: The temperature was measured at the centre of the laser hotspot by analysing the pyrometric signal emitted over a sample area of 3 × 3 µm<g id="1">2</g>.
La température a été mesurée au centre du rayon laser en analysant le signal pyrométrique émis sur une zone d'échantillon de 3 × 3 µm<g id="1">2</g>.

tu.prop: The temperature was measured at the centre of the laser hotspot by analysing the pyrometric signal emitted over a sample area of 3 × 3 µm2.
Melting criteria are based on the use of in situ X-ray diffraction; details are reported elsewhere10.

tuv.seg: Melting of samples throughout their entire thickness was induced by increasing laser power until the liquidus temperature was reached at the centre of the sample.
La fusion des échantillons sur toute leur épaisseur a été induite par l'augmentation de la puissance du laser jusqu'à ce que la température du liquidus soit atteinte au centre de l'échantillon.

tu.prop: Melting of samples throughout their entire thickness was induced by increasing laser power until the liquidus temperature was reached at the centre of the sample.
The laser power was maintained for between a couple of seconds (for six samples) and about 1 min (for three samples) before rapid quenching.

tuv.seg: Melting criteria are based on the use of <g id="2">in situ</g> X-ray diffraction; details are reported elsewhere<g id="3">10</g>.
Les critères de fusion sont déterminés à partir de l'utilisation <g id="2">in situ</g> de la diffraction des rayons X et des détails de l'expérience sont reportés ailleurs<g id="3">10</g>.

tu.prop: Melting criteria are based on the use of in situ X-ray diffraction; details are reported elsewhere10.
The use of two different heating procedures was dictated by the need to assess the significance of the artefact that chemical diffusion could produce in our samples.

tuv.seg: The laser power was maintained for between a couple of seconds (for six samples) and about 1 min (for three samples) before rapid quenching.
La puissance du laser a été maintenue entre quelques secondes (pour six échantillons) et environ 1 min (pour trois échantillons) avant une trempe rapide.

tu.prop: The laser power was maintained for between a couple of seconds (for six samples) and about 1 min (for three samples) before rapid quenching.
X-ray diffraction patterns show major NaCl contribution (Fig. 2), a sign of good thermal insulation between the laser-heated sample and the diamonds.

tuv.seg: The use of two different heating procedures was dictated by the need to assess the significance of the artefact that chemical diffusion could produce in our samples.
L'utilisation de deux procédures de chauffage différentes a été motivée par la nécessité d'évaluer l'importance de l'artefact que la diffusion chimique pourrait produire dans nos échantillons.

tu.prop: The use of two different heating procedures was dictated by the need to assess the significance of the artefact that chemical diffusion could produce in our samples.
This minimizes the axial temperature gradient in the sample.

tuv.seg: X-ray diffraction patterns show major NaCl contribution (<g id="4">Fig. 2</g>), a sign of good thermal insulation between the laser-heated sample and the diamonds.

Les diagrammes de diffraction des rayons X montrent une contribution majeure de NaCl (<g id="4">Fig. 2</g>), signe d'une bonne isolation thermique entre l'échantillon chauffé au laser et les diamants.

tu.prop: X-ray diffraction patterns show major NaCl contribution (Fig. 2), a sign of good thermal insulation between the laser-heated sample and the diamonds.
No chemical reactions between NaCl and our samples could be detected on the basis of X-ray diffraction or electron-probe microanalyses.

tuv.seg: This minimizes the axial temperature gradient in the sample.
Cela minimise le gradient de température axiale dans l'échantillon.

tu.prop: This minimizes the axial temperature gradient in the sample.
The NaCl equation of state was used to derive the pressure at room temperature31, before and after laser heating.

tuv.seg: No chemical reactions between NaCl and our samples could be detected on the basis of X-ray diffraction or electron-probe microanalyses.
Aucune réaction chimique entre le NaCl et nos échantillons n'a pu être détectée sur la base de la diffraction des rayons X ou des micro-analyses par sondes électroniques.

tu.prop: No chemical reactions between NaCl and our samples could be detected on the basis of X-ray diffraction or electron-probe microanalyses.
In this paper, we report experimental runs at nine different nominal pressures.

tuv.seg: The NaCl equation of state was used to derive the pressure at room temperature<g id="1">31</g>, before and after laser heating.
L'équation d'état du NaCl a été utilisée pour dériver la pression à température ambiante<g id="1">31</g>, avant et après le chauffage au laser.

tu.prop: The NaCl equation of state was used to derive the pressure at room temperature31, before and after laser heating.
We estimate the pressure correction (ΔP) associated with the laser heating in a partially isochoric regime using the same technique as in a previous study10.

tuv.seg: In this paper, we report experimental runs at nine different nominal pressures.
Dans cet article, nous rapportons des essais expérimentaux à neuf pressions nominales différentes.

tu.prop: In this paper, we report experimental runs at nine different nominal pressures.
This yields ΔP (GPa) = 2.5 × 10−3 ΔT(K), where ΔT = T − 300 (ΔT, change in temperature; T, temperature of sample synthesis).

tuv.seg: We estimate the pressure correction (Δ<g id="2">P</g>) associated with the laser heating in a partially isochoric regime using the same technique as in a previous study<g id="3">10</g>.

Nous estimons la correction de pression (<g id="2">ΔP</g>) associée au chauffage laser en régime partiellement isochorique en utilisant la même technique que dans une étude précédente<g id="3">10</g>.

tu.prop: We estimate the pressure correction (ΔP) associated with the laser heating in a partially isochoric regime using the same technique as in a previous study10.
On the basis of nominal pressures and experimental temperatures reported in Table 1, we recalculated the pressure encountered by each sample at high temperatures.

tuv.seg: This yields Δ<g id="4">P</g> (GPa) = 2.5 × 10<g id="5">−3</g> Δ<g id="6">T</g>(K), where Δ<g id="7">T</g> = <g id="8">T</g> − 300 (Δ<g id="9">T</g>, change in temperature; <g id="10">T</g>, temperature of sample synthesis).

Cela donne<g id="4">ΔP</g> (GPa) = 2,5 × 10<g id="5">−3</g> Δ<g id="6">T</g>(K), où Δ<g id="7">T</g> = <g id="8">T</g>− 300 (Δ<g id="9">T</g>, changement de température ; <g id="10">T</g>, température de synthèse de l'échantillon).

tu.prop: This yields ΔP (GPa) = 2.5 × 10−3 ΔT(K), where ΔT = T − 300 (ΔT, change in temperature; T, temperature of sample synthesis).

tuv.seg: On the basis of nominal pressures and experimental temperatures reported in <g id="11">Table 1</g>, we recalculated the pressure encountered by each sample at high temperatures.

Sur la base des pressions nominales et des températures expérimentales rapportées dans le <g id="11">tableau 1</g>, nous avons recalculé la pression rencontrée par chaque échantillon à des températures élevées.

tu.prop: On the basis of nominal pressures and experimental temperatures reported in Table 1, we recalculated the pressure encountered by each sample at high temperatures.
X-ray diffraction was performed at the ID27 beamline of the ESRF, simultaneously with a first set of XRF measurements (see below).

tuv.seg: X-ray diffraction
Diffraction des rayons X

tu.prop: Two under-vacuum undulators generate an X-ray flux of 8.4 × 1011 photons s−1 for an energy tuned to a wavelength of 0.3738 Å. The X-ray beam was focused by two Kirkpatrick–Baez mirrors to less than 2 µm × 2 µm full-width half-maximum spot on the sample.

tuv.seg: X-ray diffraction was performed at the ID27 beamline of the ESRF, simultaneously with a first set of XRF measurements (see below).
La diffraction des rayons X a été réalisée au niveau de la ligne de faisceau ID27 de l'ESRF, simultanément à un premier ensemble de mesures de fluorescence X (voir ci-dessous).

tu.prop: X-ray diffraction was performed at the ID27 beamline of the ESRF, simultaneously with a first set of XRF measurements (see below).
We used the MAR-160 charge-coupled-device detector with a typical acquisition time of 20–30 s. Diffraction images were analysed and integrated using the Fit2d program32.

tuv.seg: Two under-vacuum undulators generate an X-ray flux of 8.4 × 10<g id="1">11</g> photons s<g id="2">−1</g> for an energy tuned to a wavelength of 0.3738 Å. The X-ray beam was focused by two Kirkpatrick–Baez mirrors to less than 2 µm × 2 µm full-width half-maximum spot on the sample.

Deux ondulateurs sous vide génèrent un flux de rayons X de 8,4 ×10<g id="1">11</g> photons s<g id="2">−1</g> pour une énergie accordée à une longueur d'onde de 0,3738 Å. Le faisceau de rayons X a été focalisé par deux miroirs Kirkpatrick–Baez sur moins de 2 µm x 2 µm en pleine largeur à mi-hauteur sur l'échantillon.

tu.prop: Two under-vacuum undulators generate an X-ray flux of 8.4 × 1011 photons s−1 for an energy tuned to a wavelength of 0.3738 Å. The X-ray beam was focused by two Kirkpatrick–Baez mirrors to less than 2 µm × 2 µm full-width half-maximum spot on the sample.
We refined phase contents by performing multiphase Rietveld refinements using the General Structure Analysis System code33.

tuv.seg: We used the MAR-160 charge-coupled-device detector with a typical acquisition time of 20–30 s. Diffraction images were analysed and integrated using the Fit2d program<g id="3">32</g>.

Nous avons utilisé le détecteur à dispositif à transfert de charge MAR-160 avec un temps d'acquisition typique de 20 à 30 secondes. Les images de diffraction ont été analysées et intégrées à l'aide du programme<g id="3"> Fit2d32</g>.

tu.prop: We used the MAR-160 charge-coupled-device detector with a typical acquisition time of 20–30 s. Diffraction images were analysed and integrated using the Fit2d program32.
According to the composition of the starting material, proportions of Mg-Pv and Ca-bearing (Ca-Pv) perovskite phases and Fp are expected to be 75.7, 4.5 and 19.8 mol%, respectively.

tuv.seg: We refined phase contents by performing multiphase Rietveld refinements using the General Structure Analysis System code<g id="4">33</g>.
Nous avons affiné le contenu des phases en effectuant des affinements Rietveld multiphasiques à l'aide du code<g id="4">33 </g>du General Structure Analysis System.

tu.prop: We refined phase contents by performing multiphase Rietveld refinements using the General Structure Analysis System code33.

We note a weak Ca-Pv signal for some of the samples, because the Ca-Pv amorphizes partially on decompression and because rapid quenching from the molten state could lead to other Ca-rich polymorphs, such as the “new aluminous phase”34.

tuv.seg: According to the composition of the starting material, proportions of Mg-Pv and Ca-bearing (Ca-Pv) perovskite phases and Fp are expected to be 75.7, 4.5 and 19.8 mol%, respectively.
Selon la composition du matériau initial, les proportions de phases de pérovskite contenant du Mg-Pv et du Ca (Ca-Pv) et de Fp devraient être respectivement de 75,7, 4,5 et 19,8 mol %.

tu.prop: According to the composition of the starting material, proportions of Mg-Pv and Ca-bearing (Ca-Pv) perovskite phases and Fp are expected to be 75.7, 4.5 and 19.8 mol%, respectively.
We also note a weak precision for the determination of Fp contents, because its major diffraction peaks overlap with those of the major Mg-Pv phase.

tuv.seg: We note a weak Ca-Pv signal for some of the samples, because the Ca-Pv amorphizes partially on decompression and because rapid quenching from the molten state could lead to other Ca-rich polymorphs, such as the “new aluminous phase”<g id="5">34</g>.

Nous notons un faible signal Ca-Pv pour certains des échantillons, car le Ca-Pv s'amorphe partiellement à la décompression et parce qu'une trempe rapide à partir de l'état fondu pourrait conduire à d'autres polymorphes riches en Ca, comme la « nouvelle phase alumineuse »<g id="5"> 34</g>.

tu.prop: We note a weak Ca-Pv signal for some of the samples, because the Ca-Pv amorphizes partially on decompression and because rapid quenching from the molten state could lead to other Ca-rich polymorphs, such as the “new aluminous phase”34.

tuv.seg: We also note a weak precision for the determination of Fp contents, because its major diffraction peaks overlap with those of the major Mg-Pv phase.
On note également une faible précision pour la détermination des teneurs en Fp, car ses pics de diffraction majeurs se chevauchent avec ceux de la phase majeure Mg-Pv.

tu.prop: We also note a weak precision for the determination of Fp contents, because its major diffraction peaks overlap with those of the major Mg-Pv phase.

tuv.seg: To maximize the photoelectric effects and minimize Compton and Rayleigh scattering, we performed a second set of experiments in backscattering mode with the detector set at about 90° from the incoming beam.

tuv.seg: The sample-to-detector distance was about 20 mm. We detected K-lines of Ca and Fe from the sample, K-lines of Cl from the NaCl pressure medium, L-lines of W or Re from the gasket material, and L-lines of the Pb used as the X-ray absorber on the beamline.

La distance échantillon-détecteur était d'environ 20 mm. tuv.seg: This results in a less-efficient XRF count for Mg and Si, because their absorption cross sections are largest just above their 1<g id="4">s</g> binding energies (1.305 keV and 1.839 keV, respectively), and decrease as the excitation energy increases.

tu.prop: The Fe content for the liquid phase is obtained by integrating XRF intensities over the whole sphere-shell structure.
The unheated starting material can also be used as an internal standard (with Fe/(Σ cations) = 6.1%) to transform XRF intensities into atomic percentages.

tuv.seg: For the second, based on the knowledge of the XRF intensity emitted by Fe at each pixel, we obtain Fe maps for all the samples considered in this study (<g id="4">Supplementary Fig. 1</g>).

tu.prop: Superimposed on each Fe map, we draw sample areas, which include between 13 and 298 pixels for the Pv region, and between 4 and 72 pixels for the liquid regions.

tuv.seg: We average the XRF signal over these sample areas and retrieve mean values and standard deviations for XRF intensities emitted by Fe in both liquid and perovkite regions (<g id="5">Supplementary Table 1</g>).

Nous faisons la moyenne du signal XRF sur ces zones d'échantillon et récupérons les valeurs moyennes et les écarts-types pour les intensités XRF émises par le Fe dans les régions liquide et le perovkite (<g id="5">Tableau supplémentaire 1</g>).

tu.prop: We average the XRF signal over these sample areas and retrieve mean values and standard deviations for XRF intensities emitted by Fe in both liquid and perovkite regions (Supplementary Table 1).
This calculation neglects the difference in matrix corrections that should be done for the two different sample regions.

tuv.seg: In the third source of uncertainty, <g id="6">D</g><g id="7">Fe</g> values are retrieved from the ratio between Fe-XRF intensities measured in the perovskite and liquid sample regions.

Dans la troisième source d'incertitude, les valeurs de la <g id="6">D</g><g id="7">Fe</g> sont extraites du rapport entre les intensités du Fe et de la XRF mesurées dans les régions d'échantillon de pérovskite et de liquide.

tu.prop: In the third source of uncertainty, DFe values are retrieved from the ratio between Fe-XRF intensities measured in the perovskite and liquid sample regions.

The assumption seems mostly reasonable, because Fe is the heaviest element in the sample (and thus its XRF radiation is poorly reabsorbed by the sample) and because both silicate phases have comparable electronic densities.

tuv.seg: This calculation neglects the difference in matrix corrections that should be done for the two different sample regions.
Ce calcul ne tient pas compte de la différence dans les corrections de matrice qui doivent être faites pour les deux régions d'échantillon différentes.

tu.prop: This calculation neglects the difference in matrix corrections that should be done for the two different sample regions.
Further experimental uncertainty could also arise from a possible variation of sample thickness as a function of sample position.

tuv.seg: The assumption seems mostly reasonable, because Fe is the heaviest element in the sample (and thus its XRF radiation is poorly reabsorbed by the sample) and because both silicate phases have comparable electronic densities.

L'hypothèse semble surtout raisonnable, car le Fe est l'élément le plus lourd de l'échantillon (et donc son rayonnement XRF est mal réabsorbé par l'échantillon), et parce que les deux phases de silicate ont des densités électroniques comparables.

tu.prop: The assumption seems mostly reasonable, because Fe is the heaviest element in the sample (and thus its XRF radiation is poorly reabsorbed by the sample) and because both silicate phases have comparable electronic densities.

In a previous report3 of a similar experiment performed using an Ar pressure medium (instead of NaCl, as in our study), it was shown that the sample thickness is basically unchanged after laser heating.

tuv.seg: Further experimental uncertainty could also arise from a possible variation of sample thickness as a function of sample position.
Une incertitude expérimentale supplémentaire pourrait également découler d'une variation possible de l'épaisseur de l'échantillon en fonction de la position de l'échantillon.

tu.prop: Further experimental uncertainty could also arise from a possible variation of sample thickness as a function of sample position.
Our observations are also compatible with an atomic diffusion restricted to small distances.

tuv.seg: In a previous report<g id="8">3</g> of a similar experiment performed using an Ar pressure medium (instead of NaCl, as in our study), it was shown that the sample thickness is basically unchanged after laser heating.

Dans un rapport précédent<g id="8">3</g> d' une expérience similaire réalisée en utilisant un milieu sous pression Ar (au lieu de NaCl, comme dans notre étude), il a été montré que l'épaisseur de l'échantillon est fondamentalement inchangée après le chauffage au laser.

tu.prop: In a previous report3 of a similar experiment performed using an Ar pressure medium (instead of NaCl, as in our study), it was shown that the sample thickness is basically unchanged after laser heating.
This is particularly true for the six samples heated for only a few seconds, for which the shape of the original piece of glass remained unchanged (Fig. 1a).

tuv.seg: Our observations are also compatible with an atomic diffusion restricted to small distances.
Nos observations sont également compatibles avec une diffusion atomique restreinte à de petites distances.

tu.prop: Our observations are also compatible with an atomic diffusion restricted to small distances.
However, thickness variations up to 10% could be below our detection limit.

tuv.seg: This is particularly true for the six samples heated for only a few seconds, for which the shape of the original piece of glass remained unchanged (<g id="9">Fig. 1a</g>).

Cela est particulièrement vrai pour les six échantillons chauffés pendant quelques secondes seulement, pour lesquels la forme de la pièce de verre d'origine est restée inchangée (<g id="9">Fig. 1a</g>).

tu.prop: This is particularly true for the six samples heated for only a few seconds, for which the shape of the original piece of glass remained unchanged (Fig. 1a).
We therefore use this value as an uncertainty.

tuv.seg: However, thickness variations up to 10% could be below our detection limit.
Cependant, les variations d'épaisseur jusqu'à 10 % pourraient être inférieures à notre limite de détection.

tu.prop: However, thickness variations up to 10% could be below our detection limit.
Combining all the sources of uncertainty described above, we estimate the total uncertainty on DFe partition coefficients to be between 0.09 and 0.13, for DFe values between 0.47 and 0.6 (Table 1).

tuv.seg: We therefore use this value as an uncertainty.
Nous considérons donc que cette valeur est une incertitude.

tu.prop: We therefore use this value as an uncertainty.

tuv.seg: Combining all the sources of uncertainty described above, we estimate the total uncertainty on <g id="10">D</g><g id="11">Fe</g> partition coefficients to be between 0.09 and 0.13, for <g id="12">D</g><g id="13">Fe</g> values between 0.47 and 0.6 (<g id="14">Table 1</g>).

En combinant toutes les sources d'incertitude décrites ci-dessus, nous estimons que l'incertitude totale sur les coefficients de partition de la <g id="10">D</g><g id="11">Fe</g> est comprise entre 0,09 et 0,13, pour des valeurs <g id="12">D</g><g id="13">Fe</g> comprises entre 0,47 et 0,6 (<g id="14">Tableau 1</g>).
